title: The Old Man and the Sea
author: Ernest Hemingway
content: “But man is not made for defeat. A man can be destroyed but not defeated.”

title: The Grapes of Wrath
author: John Steinbeck
content: “I'll be all around in the dark. I'll be ever'-where—wherever you look. Wherever they's a fight so hungry people can eat, I'll be there.”

title: The Scarlet Letter
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
content: “Be true! Be true! Be true! Show freely to the world, if not your worst, yet some trait whereby the worst may be inferred!”

title: A Farewell to Arms
author: Ernest Hemingway
content: “The world breaks every one and afterward many are strong at the broken places.”

title: Song of Myself
author: Walt Whitman
content: “Do I contradict myself? Very well then I contradict myself, (I am large, I contain multitudes.)”
